INSIGHTS_KNOWLEDGE.title: Your Cycle, Explained
INSIGHTS_KNOWLEDGE.content:
Your cycle has four phases governed by four hormones: Estrogen, Progesterone, FSH, and LH.

Phase 1 (Menstrual): Energy is low. Rest is key.

Phase 2 (Follicular): Estrogen rises — you feel more social, creative, confident.

Phase 3 (Ovulation): Your biological peak. Highest fertility and energy.

Phase 4 (Luteal): Progesterone leads. Quieter, more introspective. PMS may occur.

INSIGHTS_KNOWLEDGE.title: Eating for Your Phases
INSIGHTS_KNOWLEDGE.content:
During your period: Iron-rich foods (lentils, spinach, red meat) replace blood loss.

Follicular phase: Fermented foods support estrogen metabolism.

Luteal phase: Magnesium is your best friend — dark chocolate and bananas reduce cramps and mood swings.

INSIGHTS_KNOWLEDGE.title: Hormones & Emotions
INSIGHTS_KNOWLEDGE.content:
Estrogen peaks correlate with higher serotonin (happiness). Progesterone rise can trigger irritability or anxiety in some.

Tracking your mood alongside your cycle helps you recognise "bad days" as biological shifts — not personal failures.

INSIGHTS_KNOWLEDGE.title: Cramps & Bloating Relief
INSIGHTS_KNOWLEDGE.content:
Heat therapy is clinically as effective as some painkillers.

Hydration: 3L of water reduces bloating by flushing retained water.

Movement: Gentle walks or yoga increase pelvic blood flow, easing uterine contractions.

menstrual.name: Menstrual
menstrual.desc: Your body is shedding and renewing. Focus on rest, warmth, and self-kindness.

follicular.name: Follicular
follicular.desc: Estrogen climbs — your brain is more creative and energised. Great for starting new things.

ovulation.name: Ovulation
ovulation.desc: Peak energy and confidence. You are at your biological best right now.

luteal.name: Luteal
luteal.desc: Progesterone rises, preparing for rest. You may feel more sensitive or introspective.
